edible_parts: Roasted heart and flower stalk ('mescal'); flowers.
other_uses: Compact silver-blue rosette succulent of the Southwest; sends up a towering flower stalk once, then dies.
prep: The starchy heart ('cabeza') and young flower stalk were pit-roasted for days into sweet, molasses-rich 'mescal' — a Southwest staple; the leaf fibers make cordage. Distilled, agave hearts make mescal/tequila-type spirits.
risks: Sap can irritate skin; leaf tips are spine-sharp.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
